entry.title: Re: deletion task /operation in pm order
entry.content:
This is not possible through standard SAP configuration. During planned maintenance order creation, the system copies all operations from the assigned task list according to the standard logic.

Alternate Solution

1. If you need to remove a specific operation dynamically, you'll need to implement a BAdI/User Exit or another enhancement during order creation.

2. Alternatively, if the operation should never be included for certain scenarios, maintaining separate task lists or maintenance strategies is the recommended standard approach.

entry.title: Re: How to post Free of Charge Delivery (BDA) inventory c...
entry.content:
• Split Valuation is not SAP’s recommended best practice for free‑of‑charge deliveries; it’s a structural option if your business already needs valuation differentiation.

  • For your requirement, SAP generally recommends using substitution/validation rules in Finance or other clean‑core extensibility to redirect postings, rather than redesigning inventory valuation.

This way, you preserve standard logistics behavior (movement type 601, CBFD/CBXN scope item) but achieve the desired accounting outcome in Finance.

entry.title: Updated Stay Certified Programs for TM and EWM
entry.content:
We are glad to announce the updates of our Stay Certified programs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editon, Extended Warehouse Management and Transportation Management. You can find the new assets (recorded live sessions) via the following links:

  • Stay Certified for EWM 
  • Stay Certified for TM 

What is Stay Certified?

Stay Certified is SAP's program for maintaining up-to-date certifications by completing periodic update learning modules and assessments designed for specific roles.

Which certifications are associated with this update?

  • The Stay Certified program for EWM is associated with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Extended Warehouse Management certification (C_S4EWM).
  • The Stay Certified program for TM is associated with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Transportation Management certification (C_S4TM).

How do I take the Stay Certified assessment?

To take the Stay Certified assessment, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to the learning.sap.com account.
  2. Click on "My Certifications" under your profile. You will then see a list of all your certifications.
  3.  Click on "Manage certification". 

 

  1. Click on the "Take Assessment" button.

Note: Access to the Stay Certified programs requires a subscription on the learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: A New-Era of Learning for Support from SAP - Support Accreditation 2026 Release is coming soon!
entry.content:
Support Accreditation has long been the foundation for enabling customers, partners, and employees to successfully engage with support from SAP.

With its 2026 release, the program undergoes its most significant evolution to date - redesigned to reflect modern learning approaches, the latest advancements in SAP’s support services, and extensive feedback from customers and learners.

Built for a new era of AI-driven, proactive, and personalized support, the updated experience focuses on one clear goal: enabling users to resolve issues faster, make better decisions, and maximize the value of SAP. 

Stay tuned for more updates coming your way in the next 10 days!

entry.title: 销售订单后续后勤活动控制
entry.content:
业务场景

1. 当创建销售订单触发审批流时，如果销售单未获批准或正处于审批流程中，则无需触发库存需求。在APP管理物料覆盖范围（及MRP有关APP）中，必须阻止或不显示该销售订单物料需求的转移。

2. 关于第三方销售订单场景，当销售订单创建且审批状态为“待审批”时，系统将生成该销售订单的采购需求。然而，若销售订单信息由多个部门共同审批，则内容可能存在修改。此时，销售订单中的交货日期、数量及其他采购相关信息需要进行调整。如果销售订单的审批状态为“待审批”时生成采购需求，此时采购部门将根据该需求向供应商发出采购订单。在审批完成后，若销售订单内容中对交货日期和数量进行了修改，则基于“待审批”需求发出的采购订单将与实际需求不符，严重影响企业的正常工作。需求控制第三方销售订单处于审批时，不生成采购申请。

3. 当销售订单被信用冻结后，要求物料需求不会被转至采购或生产环节。

无需手动操作即可将计划行类别更改为CN（无MRP）。当选择确认时的交货限制后，需求转移将被自动阻止。防止在受限订单中分配可用产品，并在确认限制期间阻止进一步的MRP（采购）处理。

系统实现

案例：Make-to-Order Processing

创建销售订单

点击行项目

字段Supply Block Reason设置Item not ready 01

此时订单总体供应链状态会是冻结

查看APP：库存物料清单MD04，发现销售订单11274物料需求没有进入

回到销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked。

回到APP:库存物料清单MD04，销售订单11274物料需求进入

运行MRP：

生成生产计划订单：

注意：

一旦计划订单创建完成，供应冻结将不再影响MRP。客户可以继续将计划订单转换为生产订单。

建议:

客户可以通过检查未完整检查状态来决定是否设置字段：Supply Block Reason。

案例：第三方销售订单

当销售订单字段Supply Block Reason设置了Item not ready 01，保存后并不会自动生成采购申请

当销售订单更改字段Supply Block Status到Not Blocked，采购申请自动创建。

 

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: Error with bank statement import
entry.content:
Hi Dmitrijs,

Short answer: This is an XSD / structure‑validation error raised while the file is parsed against the selected format mapping — not an EBS posting or bank/GL configuration problem. The incoming camt.053 contains a financial‑institution (agent) block that is present but incomplete, or the wrong camt version was selected, so the mandatory identification node can't be read. The fix lives in the file or the format mapping, not in your EBS config.

What the message means
BranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification4 is the ISO 20022 complex type that identifies a bank/agent (e.g. DbtrAgt, CdtrAgt, InstgAgt, or the account servicer Svcr) in the camt.053.001.02 generation. Its mandatory child is the financial‑institution identification, FinInstnId. "…must occur at least 1 time" is the parser saying a mandatory occurrence of that block was expected but not found. The token N00551199622 is an internal parser node reference, not a business value — ignore it.

Interpretation: The most likely cause is that one agent element in the file is opened but empty (no FinInstnId inside), or the file's version/namespace doesn't match the format you selected, so the engine can't locate the mandatory node. You have not provided me with more details, so treat the precise failing tag as something to confirm in the file rather than as a fact.

Note: Reproduce the file in Test Incoming Format Mappings (F6960) and read the Log tab — the engine shows the run as "Completed with Errors" and the log points to exactly where the structure/format check failed, without posting anything. App does not have its own tile, it needs to be accessed from Map Format Data apps. Reference: Community Portal

Verify (version match): In Manage Incoming Payment Files (F1680), confirm the chosen format matches the file's xmlns (…camt.053.001.02 vs .06/.08). Mapping/structure errors are raised during import in F1680 and are tied to the specific format‑mapping version selected; a version mismatch produces exactly these "mandatory node missing" errors. Reference: KBA 3159797

Fix path 1 — file side: Ask the bank to deliver a schema‑valid file where every agent element carries a FinInstnId (at least a BIC/BICFI, a clearing‑system member ID, or a name). An empty DbtrAgt / CdtrAgt / Svcr element is invalid per ISO 20022 and will always be rejected.

Fix path 2 — mapping side (if the bank can't change it): For a recurring or localised deviation, copy the standard camt.053 mapping in Map Format Data for Incoming Files from Banks (F5608), adjust the affected agent node so the empty/missing block is tolerated, then re‑validate in F6960 before going back to F1680. This copy‑standard‑then‑adjust‑the‑node pattern is the documented workaround for camt files that deviate from the standard structure. References: KBA 3604846 and Community Portal Article

Constraint: Country‑specific camt formats might not be accurate or available, so if this is a localised file, you build the custom mapping yourself. Reference: KBA 3672538

Summary
Treat this as a structural/file issue: the camt.053 has an empty or missing bank‑identification (FinInstnId) block, or the wrong camt version was picked in F1680. Confirm the version, reproduce in Test Incoming Format Mappings to see the exact failing node, then either get a corrected file from the bank or build a custom mapping in Map Format Data (F5608). Don't change EBS posting configuration for this.

Hope it helps.
Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
1. db/schema.cds 
namespace snakeladder;

// ── Status enum — the heart of the state machine ──────────────────────────
type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;    // Session created, not yet started
  Playing;    // It's this player's turn — roll the dice!
  Moving;     // Dice rolled, player token is being moved
  Blocked;    // Player hit a double-headed snake — skips next turn
  Finished;   // This player has reached square 100 and won
}

// ── Board square — pre-loaded with snake/ladder data ──────────────────────
entity BoardSquares {
  key square      : Integer;        // 1–100
      snakeTo     : Integer;        // 0 if no snake on this square
      ladderTo    : Integer;        // 0 if no ladder on this square
      isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;  // true if this is a double-headed snake mouth
}

// ── Game session ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
entity GameSessions {
  key ID          : UUID;
      name        : String(100);    // e.g. "Friday Afternoon Game"
      createdAt   : Timestamp;
      startedAt   : Timestamp;
      finishedAt  : Timestamp;
      winner      : Association to Players;
}

// ── Player — one row per player per session ───────────────────────────────
@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID          : UUID;
      session     : Association to GameSessions;
      name        : String(100);
      @readonly
      position    : Integer default 1;      // current square (1–100)
      @readonly
      TurnStatus  : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder   : Integer;                // 1 = goes first
      lastRoll    : Integer;               // last dice result
}
 
 srv/game-service.cds

using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService {

  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

    // Week 2 will implement these — declare them now so the model is complete
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting                  // transitions to next player in Week 3
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked                  // double-headed snake bite
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous            // restores pre-block status
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}
 
A screenshot of http://localhost:4004 showing the GameService endpoints

One sentence explaining your choice of TurnStatus enum values — did you add any of your own?

Kept the TurnStatus as it is, as i think its sufficient.

entry.title: Withholding Tax calculation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have quite a trouble understanding the logic (or probably we have missed a set up) that caused this computation. 

This is from two items in the billing document, and I am now aware that the SD and FI conditions/computations are not the same. However, I would like to know how the "Taxes on Sales/Purchases Amount" was derived or computed from or is it a configuration set up. The taxes on sales applied to this transaction is 12% but here the amount shows about only 2% (for both items) though they have two different WHT tax types assigned to each item. 

Can someone explain the logic/computation behind this? 

The sample expected computation is: 

Gross amount                       230,777.12
Less Taxes on Sales            - 24,726.12 (12% of 206,051)
Net amount                           206,051.00
Withholding Tax Rate                       20%
Withholding Amount           41,210.20

Also, as I was simulating another scenario trying to understand the computation logic, I created a transaction that had 230,777.12 as the gross amount still, upon simulation, the gross amount shows 1,000 (I have condition record PPR0 set up for 1,000 for this but I changed the amount in the transaction to 230,777.12).

I would like to know how the system derives these amounts. 
Thank you for your patience and help!

entry.title: Customize Your Emails for Situation Handling in the Extended Framework
entry.content:
Situation Handling

Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud alerts users about issues requiring their attention. You can use SAP-delivered templates or create your own use cases to detect critical business issues.

With the extended framework for Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, you can customize the emails for object-based situations. This blog post explains the default notification settings and the customizing option.

Alert the right users about situations

Situation Handling uses multiple channels to inform the users about critical business situations that require their attention. This includes:

  • Situation descriptions embedded in business apps
  • Notification channels in SAP Fiori launchpad and emails

Situation Handling is designed to keep users well informed and support them in their daily work.

Situation Handling emails

One way of informing users about critical business situations is by email. Emails are triggered together with notifications and sent automatically when situations occur, provided the notification option is activated.

Activate emails together with notifications

Let’s look at the standard notification settings of a situation type and their impact on emails:

  • Notify all after creation / Processor only after update: An email is sent to all recipients when a situation has occurred and to the processor when a situation was updated
  • No notification after closing: No email is sent to anybody
  • Notify all after reopening: An email is sent to all recipients

You can also define custom notification settings and their related impact. Whenever a notification is sent, an email is sent as well.

Email templates are predefined

Situation emails use predefined templates that are automatically assigned to the situation type. This means no additional configuration is required.

The predefined email template consists of the email subject and the email body. Most parts of the email body are fixed and cannot be changed. This contains the greeting, the links to SAP Fiori launchpad and to detailed situation information as well as the source and the sign-off. The customizable email text is the one that describes the situation.

Adapt email subject and email text

You have two options to adapt the email template.

  • Reuse the notification title as email subject and the notification text in the email body.
  • Add a specific plain text to the email body and go with an SAP-defined email subject.

The situation type automatically inherits the setting of the situation template. You can change the settings in the situation type at any time. Afterwards, the new emails are generated according to the updated configuration.

If you change a situation template later, it has no effect on existing situation types.

Reuse notification title and text in email subject and body

You can reuse the notification title as the email subject, including variables such as plant, supplier, or contract ID. The reuse option also inserts the notification text into the email body, where variables can be used as well. The email preview shows how the email will look like.

Additionally, you can select if notifications are aggregated or not.

  • No Aggregation: Send a notification and email for every situation instance.
  • Aggregate by Field: Group notifications as well as emails by a selected field, for instance, supplier.
  • Aggregate All: Group all notifications as well as all emails.

Let’s check how the different settings affect the emails.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

If you choose non-aggregated notifications, a separate notification and an email are sent for each situation. Initially, notification title and text are already copied from the situation title and text. Title and text can be also reused as the email subject and in the email body. This provides an efficient configuration experience.

The email template contains links to both, the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Reuse option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Reuse works the same way for aggregated notifications. Whether you aggregate all notifications or aggregate them by field, the title and text are copied to the email template’s subject and body.

For a meaningful representation, you can use only character-based variables such as strings. In addition, the count variable indicates how many situations are aggregated. If you select Aggregate by Field, you can use the aggregated by variable to show the field used for aggregation.

Because of the aggregation, the email template no longer includes a link to a specific situation; instead, it shows the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Reuse option: aggregated email by field

Aggregation by field allows more specific end user texts by including this field as variable.

If you aggregate all notifications and emails, the texts will be more generic.

Reuse option: aggregated email for all situations

Note: Be aware of the variables you are using in the email. They may contain personal data. Review the variables to prevent unintended exposure in emails. Also make sure that the data is managed in accordance with the legal requirements and business needs.

Specific email text with SAP-defined email subject

Alternatively, you can add plain texts without variables in the email body and use a default subject defined by SAP. This option makes sure that the email does not include business-sensitive data or personal data which may conflict with legal requirements or business needs.

You also get a preview of the email with this option. Let’s take a look at how this works in detail.

Non-aggregated notifications and emails

For non-aggregated notifications, an email is sent for each situation using the default subject and the embedded custom email text.

The email template includes links to both the SAP Fiori launchpad and the specific situation.

Custom option: non-aggregated email

Aggregated notifications and emails

Aggregated emails also have a default subject and a custom email text without variables.

Because the emails are aggregated, you no longer see the link to a specific situation in the email template but the generic link to SAP Fiori launchpad.

Custom option: aggregated email by field

Since the custom email texts do not contain variables, they remain generic for both aggregation options.

Custom option: aggregated email for all situations

Now you are all set and know how you can configure emails matching your business needs.

Further Information

You find more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Platform
entry.content:
Ready to accelerate your certification journey?

Join the Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Platform and sharpen the knowledge that matters in just 90 minutes.

We'll cover the certification essentials, including:
• SAP Business AI Platform and its strategic pillars.
• The Autonomous Enterprise vision.
• The value of AI agents.
• How SAP Business Technology Platform connects AI, data, and integration to drive business innovation.

entry.title: Re: Centralize Authorization Management of SAP Cloud ALM Using the Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP documentation says:
"If you're working with role mapping to user groups in your identity provider, you need to assign role collections in the SAP BTP cockpit. In this case, you can't view or change roles in the User Management app and use the role request and assignment features in SAP Cloud ALM, so we don't recommend this method."

However, I just tested this setup:

  1. Created a group in IAS (CloudALM_x_calm_GlobalAdministrator).
  2. Provisioned the group and users via IPS to the BTP Cockpit.
  3. The Users from this Groups  automatically received  the  role  x_calm_GlobalAdministrator in BTP 
  4. After about 24 hours, the roles appeared in the Cloud ALM App.

My question:
Even though I use IAS groups and IPS provisioning, I can still edit the roles in the Cloud ALM App.

 

When exactly does the Cloud ALM app block role editing? Why is role editing still possible in my setup?

As far as I understand, this is the only disadvantage of using IPS provisioning.

Thanks for you very much for your help and support.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Regarding ATP verification based on variant selection
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

Yes, with one important qualification. In the standard make-to-order process with Variant Configuration, ATP is normally performed for the top-level configurable product entered in the sales order or for the matched material variant if Variant Matching is used

Configuration-dependent BOM components are not checked individually by sales-order ATP. Components that exist only in the production BOM can be checked later during production planning or production-order processing but this component check does not automatically determine the sales order confirmation date

For standard ATP the main requirements are:

  • 2LN – Basic Available-to-Promise Processing
  • an Availability Check value maintained in the product master
  • the appropriate scope of check configured for the checking group and checking rule
  • sales document and schedule-line settings that trigger the availability check and where required the transfer of requirements

For make-to-order production with Variant Configuration the relevant scope item is 1YT. Scope item 1JW – Advanced Order Promising is only required for additional aATP functions and does not provide a multilevel ATP check of configuration-dependent BOM components

 

More details below:

SAP Help – Order Promising (2LN)

SAP Help – Advanced Order Promising (1JW)

SAP Learning Introducing Basic Available-to-Promise

SAp Leraning Managing Make to Order Process for Product Variants

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Hi All,

Here is my Week 2 submission.

srv/game-service.js

const cds = require('@sap/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {
    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async () => {
      // CAP validates @from and applies @to for this state-only transition.
    })

    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      const newPosition = player.position + roll

      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', from: newPosition, to: finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', from: newPosition, to: finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', from: newPosition, to: finalPosition }
      }

      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async () => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation. CAP applies @to: #Waiting for now.
    })

    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async () => {
      // CAP restores the previous status through @to: $flow.previous.
    })

    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      if (player.position !== 100) {
        return req.reject(409, `Player must be on square 100 to win. Current position is ${player.position}.`)
      }

      await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        .set({
          finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          winner_ID: player.ID
        })
        .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    return super.init()
  }
}

HTTP 409 proof when calling rollDice in the wrong state:


Postman screenshot showing 409 Conflict for rollDice called while the player is already in Moving state.

POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/game/Players(ID=44444444-4444-4444-4444-444444444444)/rollDice

HTTP/1.1 409 Conflict
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Action \"rollDice\" requires \"TurnStatus\" to be \"[\"Playing\"]\".",
    "code": "INVALID_FLOW_TRANSITION_SINGLE",
    "@Common.numericSeverity": 4
  }
}

Snake/ladder proof from my run:

 

Postman screenshot showing the successful rollDice response with "event": "ladder" and "position": 31.

{
  "@odata.context": "../$metadata#GameService.return_GameService_Players_rollDice",
  "roll": 4,
  "position": 31,
  "event": "ladder"
}

The player landed on square 8 and the ladder moved the token up to square 31.

If winGame is called while the player is not on square 100, my handler rejects the request with HTTP 409 and does not mark the session as finished.

entry.title: Re: New Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management and SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @kumareshan_S,

Sorry for my late reply.

Step by step:

  1. You have created the SAP BTP destination in the SAP Cloud ALM subaccount pointing to your CTMS instance as described here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/cloud-transport-management-service?locale=en-US#procedure
  2. You have create a system group for product "SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)". The list of supported products can be found here in the section "CTMS Transport Assignment Restriction".
  3. Select the corresponding tenants for CPI and "assign Transport Node".

Please let me know whether this solved your issue.

Best,

Moritz

entry.title: Re: Not able to post posting difference in PI process
entry.content:
Hello @murali_dasarid4eb32 

Check the note 3721043 - Error message "Item is inconsistent/missing in /SCWM/QUAN; GUID_PARENT &1, GUID_STOCK &2" (/SCWM/UI_DIFF 055) shows up in Analyze Differences app:

Cause
There is a database inconsistency in /SCWM/QUAN table combined with the physical inventory items.

Resolution
The inconsistency can only be corrected by SAP Support at backend.

Create a case to SAP under the component SCM-EWM-PI-2CL to correct the inconsistency.

You need to touch base with SAP support and let them clear the inconsistencies.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer. In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool. The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

If you want to get an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 and surrounding solutions, check out the SAP Cloud ERP Early Release Series.

This series will showcase live sessions led by product experts across the SAP Cloud ERP portfolio on a broad range of innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Enjoy expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A. Read this blog post to learn more.

All sessions will be accessible via the SAP Learning Hub (access for SAP Customers and Partners. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login.). You can register for upcoming sessions via the registration page. All recordings of past events will be available here.

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Async Push Notifications for Long-Running Agents
entry.content:
@felixbartler 

First of all, thank you very much for this detailed explanation of the needed config and coding. Based on this (with Claude) I could implement a working async mode. 

I would have some questions though:

1. I still experienced some kind of request time out in Joule. Claude explanation was the following (I cannot decide whether it is right or not):

What Async Solves vs What It Doesn't

Problem                                                      Sync             Async

HTTP connection timeout (30-60s)❌Breaks✓ Solved — agent acks immediately
Joule UI display timeout❌Breaks❌Also breaks (different timeout)
SAP BTP gateway timeout❌Breaks✓ Solved
Result lost if user closes browser❌Lost✓ Delivered when ready

Is this "Joule UI display timeout" really a problem even with async or it shouldn't be and I missed something?

2. The Provided APIs/API Permission Groups topic is not really clear for me: How it should be used in production? How it should be practically built up/structured?  In BTP admin for a Cloud Identity Service instance parameters can be added  like the followings: 

    "provided-apis": [
        {
            "description": "Test Data Creation A2A agent",
            "name": "TDC_a2a_agent",
            "scopes": [
                {
                    "description": "Read access to the A2A agent",
                    "name": "api_read_access"
                },
                {
                    "description": "Write access to the A2A agent",
                    "name": "api_write_access"
                }
            ]
        }
    ],

There are scopes under the APIs. Remark: this was done by me when I tried different things and most probably not correct, but the update didn't fail when I added these, so I assume these could be used like this, but the purpose is not clear compared to the IAS App API configs. So, do you know any documentation about API config what would explain how to use these?

entry.title: Turn Business Needs into SAP Solutions: Start with This Business Analyst Path
entry.content:
Turn Business Needs into SAP Solutions: Start with This Business Analyst Path 

The SAP Business Analyst role is one of the most valuable on any implementation project: bridging business stakeholders and technical teams, mapping processes, identifying gaps, and making sure that the solution fits the need. Demand for people who can do this well in an SAP context is consistently high. 

Looking to move into this space? The SAP Business Analyst Professional Certificate offers a structured starting point, no prior SAP experience required. 

What the Coursera certificate covers 

The four courses move from foundational concepts to hands-on practice with SAP solutions. Learners cover business analysis fundamentals and how SAP Activate applies to the Business Analyst (BA) role, process modelling with SAP Signavio, data analysis and reporting with SAP Analytics Cloud, and applied business analysis through real-world transformation case studies. Guided access to trial versions of SAP Signavio and SAP Analytics Cloud is included as part of the Coursera certificate. It also carries ACE (American Council on Education) credit recommendations, meaning learners can apply their learning toward academic credit at participating U.S. and Latin American universities. 

Who this is for 

The certificate is a strong starting point for people looking to move into the BA role in an SAP context. It's also useful for people already working in adjacent roles like project coordinators, functional leads, software users who want to build a foundation before pursuing deeper SAP training and certification.  

From Coursera certificate to SAP Certification 

This Coursera certificate builds the foundation. From there, learners deepen their expertise on SAP Learning site through self-paced courses and expert-led journeys. 

Get started on Coursera now: SAP Business Analyst Professional Certificate

entry.title: What's new in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release
entry.content:
When you trace the evolution of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, a pattern emerges: every release compounds our momentum; continuous innovation on a solid core.

We’ve broadened business scope, deepened our ecosystem, simplified upgrades, and scaled across industries and regions. With the release of 2608, we’re taking the next confident step.

Introducing the Autonomous Enterprise at SAP Sapphire, Business AI moves from concept to companion (you are welcome to follow the link Autonomous Enterprise | SAP) — not a driver, but an intelligent co‑worker embedded in the ERP experience. It helps the business users work faster and smarter while they stay firmly in control. The best decisions still come from people; AI simply helps them get there sooner.

Marking our 35th major release in SaaS ERP, we’re pairing Business AI with continued substantive progress across the core of the business: Finance and Manufacturing, Engineering and Workplace Safety, and Professional Services. All that keeps our customers’ business moving.

 

As always, I wish I had more time to show you everything we've delivered in this release. So, take this video as an appetizer for our upcoming deep-dive blogs, where my colleagues and I will walk you through many more innovations across various lines of business.

 

 

SAP-managed scenarios

Before we start with Business AI, let me share some of the latest capabilities around SAP-managed scenarios. I believe this is something that delivers significant value to every customer who adopts more than just core finance processes, which is the case for most of our customers. We started introducing SAP-managed scenarios with 2602 and continue to expand the portfolio with additional scenarios, where SAP takes care of the setup and interoperability across the suite of solutions. Our Suite Product Management team has done a fantastic job in this area, delivering the first scenarios with big success.

Since the 2602 release, Early Adopter customers and partners have successfully adopted the first SAP-managed business scenarios:

  • SAP-managed Workforce Management enables seamless Hire to Retire processes based on consistent workforce and financial data across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors.
  • SAP-managed Operational Procurement provides a connected Procure to Pay experience through SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Ariba Buying, and SAP Ariba Invoicing.

More SAP-managed innovation with 2608:

Enhancement of SAP-managed Hire to Retire

  • Expansion of SAP-managed Hire to Retire with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding and SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, extending end-to-end process coverage across the employee lifecycle.
  • Consolidated Time Recording for Professional Services, improving the user experience for time and activity recording.
  • Contingent Workers Management across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors throughout the worker lifecycle.

Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed Lead to Cash

  • Early Adopter Care for SAP-managed integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, enabling a connected Sales Force Automation process with consistent data, improved sales visibility, and a seamless experience across front-office and back-office operations.

Strengthening SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core

  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Subscription Billing and SAP Market Rates Management. Embedded into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user experience, these capabilities enable seamless cross-solution processes from a single launchpad.
  • SAP-managed integration with SAP Taulia extends working capital management processes, helping customers reduce integration complexity and connect finance and treasury operations more seamlessly across solutions.


But now, let's move on to Business AI. Across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we're introducing specialized AI assistants and agents that automate routine tasks, provide intelligent recommendations, and support users in their everyday work. Here are a few highlights that stood out to me.

 

Business AI innovations

Before we dig deeper into the topic of individual Business AI innovations, let me make a clarifying statement.

Business AI and Business users are designed to be a duet, not a duel.

AI agents are becoming an increasingly important part of our product evolution. We are seeing an increasing number of specialized AI agents supporting business tasks, while users remain firmly in control of business decisions and approvals. Agents help automate repetitive activities, provide intelligent recommendations, and work together behind the scenes to make everyday business processes more efficient. This lifts the execution level while the business user is still in control. This very approach, combining intelligent automation with human expertise, you will see reflected throughout the 2608 release.

Here are a few agents that I am excited about in the 2608 release:

 

SAP User Experience

AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

We want everyone to feel at home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so we’re making the user experience easier to learn and quicker to master. That’s why we created Fast with SAP Fiori: short, one‑minute videos that show you exactly how to get things done, from working with tables to setting default values. You can find the first 10 tips right in the Help menu of the SAP Fiori launchpad, with more on the way. They’re easy to watch, easy to share from the SAP Digital Library, and available in different languages. Don’t see the tip you need? Just send us an improvement request in the SAP CIP session “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross‑Topics.”

We’re also expanding our AI-assisted UX. I am happy to announce the general availability of the initial version of AI-assisted easy fill. Business users often spend unnecessary time transferring information from emails, meeting notes, or phone calls into ERP transactions—an effort that is both repetitive and prone to manual errors. Users can now simply describe the business update in natural language. SAP automatically populates the relevant fields, making data entry faster, more intuitive, and less prone to errors. It's a great example of how Business AI is simplifying everyday work and making SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition easier to use.

This feature is one of 17 base AI-assisted features available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608. If not yet done, make sure to sign the "Acceptance of SAP AI terms for Base AI-assisted features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" to use the generally available base features at no additional cost. 

If you want to be part of the beta testing of the latest AI innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, kindly sign-up here. 

 

Finance & Risk

Receivables & Payables Clearing Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Let’s continue with Finance. Anyone who worked in this function knows how much time disappears into matching and clearing open receivables and payables. With our new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent, that work changes fundamentally. It analyses open items, proposes balanced clearing entries, explains every recommendation, and, once approved, supports automated posting. The outcome: a faster, more accurate clearing process that lifts finance productivity and helps shorten the financial close.

 

International Trade Classification Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Classifying products for international trade is a complex and highly regulated process that requires significant expertise and careful interpretation of customs regulations. The International Trade Classification Agent leverages Business AI to analyze product information, interpret trade regulations, and recommend appropriate customs classification codes together with the legal reasoning behind each recommendation. This helps trade specialists classify products more efficiently, create auditable decisions, reduce compliance risks, and strengthen supply chain resilience while significantly reducing the effort required for classification.

 

Statutory Report Analysis Agent (planned for 2608.2)

Preparing statutory reports often requires finance teams to manually compare reporting periods, investigate unexpected variances, and trace the root causes of unusual values across tax codes, business partners, and financial documents. The new Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps finance organizations review statutory reports faster, identify anomalies earlier, and improve compliance before submission. The AI agent automates this analysis by detecting outliers, identifying trends and irregularities, generating concise summaries of key findings, and recommending next steps for further investigation or validation. By reducing manual review effort and providing explainable, data-driven insights, the Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps organizations improve reporting accuracy, accelerate the declaration process, identify potential compliance risks earlier, and make more informed decisions before submitting statutory reports.

 

 

E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent (planned for 2608.2)

As e-invoicing mandates continue to expand worldwide, finance teams increasingly need to process and troubleshoot submission errors returned by tax authorities and e-invoicing networks under tight regulatory deadlines. The E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent helps organizations quickly identify, understand, and resolve errors during electronic invoice submission. The AI agent automatically classifies technical and business-related errors, distinguishes between infrastructure, connectivity, data quality, and financial posting issues, and provides guided root cause analysis together with contextual resolution recommendations. By standardizing error handling, reducing manual investigation, and delivering explainable recommendations with humans in the loop, the agent helps organizations accelerate issue resolution, improve compliance with government e-invoicing requirements, and reduce the risk of submission delays and regulatory penalties.

 

Manufacturing

Production Planning & Operations Agent

Manufacturing is another space where AI moves the needle. Our new Production Planning and Operations Agent equips supervisors to manage orders with greater confidence: it automatically checks component availability, flags scheduling conflicts, and even supports order release via natural language. By cutting routine manual work and surfacing the right insights at the right time, it lifts productivity, reduces downtime, and keeps operations running smoothly.

 

Further innovations across various LoBs

SAP Integrated Product Development

Handover from Formulation Recipe to S/4HANA Master Recipe

Bringing new products from development into manufacturing often requires manual handovers and multiple data transfers, increasing the risk of inconsistencies and delays. Handover to Master Recipe in SAP Integrated Product Development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition streamlines this transition by enabling production engineers to derive production-ready master recipes directly from product development data. The innovation automates the creation of master recipes, bills of materials (BOMs), and production versions while preserving the relationship between engineering and manufacturing data. By reducing manual effort and ensuring greater consistency across product development and production planning, organizations can accelerate manufacturing readiness, improve process reliability, and support a more seamless transition from product design to execution. For more information, consult our SAP Help Portal.

 

Sales

Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities

A single sales order can trigger a cascade of downstream activities, from available-to-promise (ATP) checks and material requirements planning (MRP) to logistics execution. Supply block in a sales order to control subsequent activities helps organizations ensure these processes only begin when order data is complete, validated, and ready for execution. This innovation introduces an automated, item-level supply block that can be configured based on business rules, approval workflows, credit checks, or custom validation logic. By temporarily pausing subsequent activities until all required validations have been completed, organizations can reduce operational risk, minimize manual intervention, improve visibility into order status, and help ensure more accurate, reliable, and efficient order fulfillment.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

Maintain and view hazardous substance inventory

Another innovation focuses on workplace safety and regulatory compliance. The new Maintain and View Hazardous Substance Inventory applications provide a centralized view of hazardous substances, associated products, and safety information across the enterprise. Users can create and maintain comprehensive hazardous substance inventories, organize data by business area or location, manage inventory revisions, and quickly access key details such as hazardous ingredients and Globally Harmonized System (GHS) classifications. This helps organizations strengthen compliance, improve workplace safety, and manage safety-critical information more efficiently.

 

Professional Services

New and improved time-recording app for professional services projects with a smooth mobile experience

Let’s close with professional services. The redesigned Time Recording app brings a modern, consistent experience across desktop and mobile, with flexible entry, bulk edits, and personalized views that make logging project time fast and effortless. The payoff is clear: more accurate timesheets, less administrative work, and stronger billing and resource planning.

   

                                         

 

Service

Customer and product hierarchy pricing

Every customer expects repair pricing to be transparent, accurate, and aligned with the agreements they have negotiated. Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy Related Pricing in In-House Repair Transactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 enables organizations to apply pricing and discounts consistently across in-house repair processes based on customer and product hierarchies. By automatically determining the appropriate pricing conditions for different customer groups and product structures, the innovation eliminates manual pricing adjustments and helps ensure that negotiated agreements are applied correctly throughout the repair process. The result is more accurate invoicing, greater pricing consistency, an improved customer experience, and a smoother connection between service execution and billing.

 

Cross Topics – Data and Analytics

Central governance of product data and business partners including customer and supplier segments

High-quality master data is the foundation of every successful ERP system. The central governance of product data and business partners helps organizations establish trusted product, customer, supplier, and business partner data through workflow-driven governance, configurable validation rules, automated field value derivation, and flexible approval processes powered by SAP Build Process Automation. By improving data quality from the point of entry and reducing manual maintenance, organizations can streamline governance, strengthen compliance, and enable more reliable business processes and better decision-making across the enterprise.

 

 

Smart business runtime environment

The Smart business runtime environment introduces AI-generated summaries that help business users understand KPIs and reports faster. Business users often spend significant time analyzing dashboards, drilling down into reports, and interpreting large volumes of business data before they can identify trends or make informed decisions. With the Smart Business Runtime Environment, users can automatically generate concise, contextual summaries for SAP Smart Business KPIs and reports directly within SAP Fiori, highlighting key insights, anomalies, and important business trends. By transforming complex analytics into clear, shareable narratives, the innovation accelerates insight consumption, simplifies data interpretation, and enables faster, more informed decision-making across the organization.

 

Closing thoughts

Every release is an opportunity to make SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition smarter, simpler, and more valuable for our customers.

On one hand, we’re expanding the possibilities of Business AI with intelligent assistants and specialized agents. On the other, we’re doubling down on the core capabilities that help organizations run better every day.

Those above are my key innovations in the 2608 Release Highlights. I’m keen to hear what matters most to you.

I hope this introduction gave you some appetizers of what’s new — and spotlighted a few innovations you’ll want to explore further. For deeper insights, check out the “What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” SAP Help page and join the conversation on SAP Community to share which innovations you’re most excited about.

In the coming weeks, we'll take a closer look at additional business- and industry-specific innovations in a series of follow-up posts here on SAP Community.  To stay up to date with future content, be sure to follow the PSCC_Enablement tag and keep an eye on upcoming posts.

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze in the community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Until next time,

Bert

 

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM SAP Data center's - EU01 ?
entry.content:
Hello Karthik,

All the supported Data Centers of Cloud ALM can be found in  help portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/supported-data-centers?locale=en-US&version=LATEST. EU01 is not referenced in the current SAP Cloud ALM supported data center list. 

EU01 is an SAP-managed data center (not hyperscaler-based, older infrastructure). Cloud ALM has generally been deployed on hyperscaler infrastructure (AWS/Azure via BTP), not the classic EU01 SAP data center.
There is no information that EU01 will be planned for Cloud ALM as it's not referenced in SAP Road Map Explorer.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts

I can completely understand your frustration. If someone is willing to pay for an SAP Learning Hub subscription as an individual, the purchasing process should be straightforward and user-friendly. 
I can also see how the requirement to enter company details can be confusing for individuals, whether you're an independent consultant, student, job seeker, or simply investing in your professional development. And waiting more than two weeks for a ticket update certainly doesn't help. 
To help you get the best visibility and support, I'd encourage you to post this question in the Q&A area rather than as a comment on the Welcome blog. The Q&A section is the best place., both SAP experts and community members are more likely to see and respond there. 
If you do create a Q&A post, please consider including your support ticket number (if you're comfortable sharing it). This can help SAP moderators and support teams look into the case more efficiently and may also help community members provide more targeted guidance. 
Hopefully, you'll get a resolution soon, and thank you for bringing your experience to the community.

Kind regards,
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Key Fields from a Released CDS View i...
entry.content:
Yes ,

But CL_ABAP_TYPEDESCR and lo_struct->get_components() describe the ABAP type/structure as it exists in the runtime type system. For CDS view entities, the “key” is semantic metadata in the DDL (it’s not enforced as a database primary key and isn’t part of the ABAP structure’s RTTI)
Use the CDS View Entity metadata model. This API exposes the DDL model of a view entity, including which elements are defined with the key keyword.

entry.title: Re: Partial settlement in condition contract management
entry.content: Hi Kai, Please check your Pricing procedure(Settlement document) configurations. Check if Standard accruals group condition is configured. Check accruals reversal condition type is listed at condition contract type settings.

entry.title: Re: HOW TO TRANSFER THE MATERIAL FROM COST CENTER (K) TO...
entry.content: You cannot directly “move” stock from a cost center (account assignment K) back into inventory, because the goods receipt with account assignment K is treated as consumption and not valuated stock. Once posted, it updates expense accounts, not inventory. The SAP‑recommended way is to reverse the consumption posting and re‑receive into inventory, or use a special movement type like 202 or 411 depending on your business rules.

entry.title: Crystal Reports - Multiple Parameters in SAP B1 Web Client
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Is there any limitation for multiple parameters in SAP Business One - Web Client? Just in case, we are running FP2602 (MSSQL).

I've created a report with two parameters: JobCode and CurrencyOption. In the Desktop client, the report runs smoothly prompting all parameters set, whereas in the Web client it does not show the Currency parameter (which was the last one to come in) and throws an error when running: "An error has occurred while generating the PDF file".

Inspecting the browser, I see that CurrencyOption is being passed as null (as expected - there's no prompt to enter it)

Is this a WC limitation or is there a different way to implement multiple parameters here?

I'd appreciate your thoughts.

Best regards
Felipe

entry.title: Re: BADI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE not triggered when th...
entry.content:
Hi @wolfgang_lindner2 ,
Could you please explain why you need an additional call of the BAdI after the delivery block is removed on header level? Does the requested delivery date depend on the date when the delivery block is removed? If yes, how do you want to determine that date? 

In general, the BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE is only called if the Item is processed completely which is not the case if the delivery block is removed.

There are two potential solutions:

1. Raise a feature request via Customer Influence Portal  and request the "Delivery Block" to be added to the "Reprocessing of Sales Document Items Based on Header Data Changes" BAdI. With that, you could define that all items get reprocessed in case the Delivery Block has been changed on header level.


2. Use "Modification of Custom Fields in Sales Documents at End of Finalize Phase Before Saving" BAdI to determine the change of the Delivery Block (compare current Delivery Block with the one on the database which you could get via select on I_SalesDocument)  and do a dummy change to the item which should be reprocessed. This will reprocess the item and the BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE will be called. Disadvantage of that approach is, that you only see the result after saving the sales document.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: How to create Business Partner Master Data to S4 Hana...
entry.content:
From the screenshots, it appears you are looking at SAP Extended Withholding Tax (EWT) calculation details during billing/invoice posting. The key point is that the "Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount" shown there is not necessarily the full VAT amount from SD pricing. It is the amount that SAP is subtracting from the withholding tax base according to the withholding tax configuration.
The Withholding Tax (WHT) is being calculated on the Net Amount shown in the analysis screen, not on the amount you expected.

Gross Amount = 230,777.12
Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount = 4,816.79
Net Amount = 225,960.33
WHT = 225,960.33 × 20% = 45,192.07 ,which matches the posted WHT amount.

The reason the Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount is only 4,816.79 (instead of the full 12% VAT of 24,726.12) is most likely due to withholding tax configuration. SAP is not deducting the full VAT from the WHT base; it is using a configured tax amount/formula to determine the taxable base.

From the screenshots, it appears you are looking at SAP Extended Withholding Tax (EWT) calculation details during billing/invoice posting. The key point is that the "Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount" shown there is not necessarily the full VAT amount from SD pricing. It is the amount that SAP is subtracting from the withholding tax base according to the withholding tax configuration.
The Withholding Tax (WHT) is being calculated on the Net Amount shown in the analysis screen, not on the amount you expected.

Gross Amount = 230,777.12
Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount = 4,816.79
Net Amount = 225,960.33
WHT = 225,960.33 × 20% = 45,192.07 ,which matches the posted WHT amount.

The reason the Tax on Sales/Purchases Amount is only 4,816.79 (instead of the full 12% VAT of 24,726.12) is most likely due to withholding tax configuration. SAP is not deducting the full VAT from the WHT base; it is using a configured tax amount/formula to determine the taxable base.
